kelly.negativeEdge: Negative edge - do not trade this strategy
kelly.highPotential: High potential but risky - Use Quarter Kelly
kelly.reasonable: Reasonable Kelly - Consider Half Kelly for growth
kelly.conservative: Conservative Kelly - Quarter Kelly recommended for stability

v2.title: Capital Expectancy Simulator
v2.subtitle: Day-aware Monte Carlo simulation respecting your decision tree
v2.capitalExplanation: This simulation applies your risk management profile's position sizing (compression cycle) to show expected dollar returns. Your profile's risk decay model determines how edge converts into capital growth.

params.budgetExceeded: Budget exceeded. Reduce simulations, trading days, or months.

analysis.title: Trading Strategy Analysis
analysis.mostLikelyOutcome: This is your most likely outcome
analysis.simulationsProfitable: {percent}% of all simulations were profitable, indicating {quality}.
analysis.profitQualityRisky: a risky strategy that needs improvement
analysis.reliableStrategy: A reliable strategy should be profitable in at least 70% of simulations.
analysis.commissionCostsNegligible: Commission costs are negligible relative to your edge.
analysis.commissionCostsModerate: Commission costs are moderate — consider negotiating lower rates.
analysis.commissionCostsHigh: Commission costs significantly erode your edge.
analysis.bestStrategyTip: The best strategy is one you can execute consistently with confidence.

tooltips.sharpeRatio: Measures risk-adjusted return by comparing average return to its volatility. Above 1.0 = good, above 2.0 = excellent. Below 0.5 suggests returns don't justify the risk taken.
tooltips.sortinoRatio: Like Sharpe but only penalizes downside volatility (losses), ignoring upside variance. Above 1.0 = good, above 2.0 = strong. Better than Sharpe for strategies with asymmetric returns.
tooltips.kellyCriterion: Mathematical formula that calculates the optimal fraction of capital to risk per trade to maximize long-term growth. Full Kelly is aggressive — most traders use Half or Quarter Kelly for smoother equity curves.
tooltips.kellyQuarter: Conservative: 25% of Full Kelly. Slower growth but much smoother equity curve. Best for risk-averse traders or unproven strategies.
tooltips.kellyHalf: Balanced: 50% of Full Kelly. Good trade-off between growth and drawdown control. Most commonly recommended for experienced traders.
tooltips.kellyFull: Aggressive: Maximum growth rate but with severe drawdowns. Mathematically optimal but psychologically difficult. Rarely used in practice.
tooltips.profitFactor: Total winning R divided by total losing R. Above 1.0 = profitable. Above 1.5 = solid edge. Above 2.0 = strong system. Below 1.0 means the system loses money overall.
tooltips.expectedRPerTrade: Your mathematical edge per trade in R-multiples. Positive = profitable system. Example: +0.3R means you gain 0.3 units of risk per trade on average over many trades.
tooltips.maxDrawdown: Largest peak-to-trough decline in R-multiples. Measures worst-case pain. Lower is better. A 5R drawdown means losing 5x your risk unit from peak before recovering.
tooltips.profitableSimulations: Percentage of Monte Carlo runs ending in profit. Above 80% = robust edge. 60-80% = moderate confidence. Below 60% = strategy may not be reliable.
tooltips.profitableMonths: Percentage of simulated months ending positive. Above 70% = consistent. 50-70% = moderate. Below 50% suggests frequent losing months.
tooltips.monthlyLimitHit: How often the monthly loss limit was triggered. Below 10% = well-managed risk. Above 20% = strategy frequently hits safety stops.
tooltips.expectedDailyPnl: Average daily profit/loss across all simulations. Accounts for position sizing, commission costs, and the compression cycle from your risk profile.
tooltips.riskOfRuin: Percentage of simulations where balance dropped to or below the ruin threshold. Lower is better.
tooltips.ruinThreshold: Account loss percentage that constitutes 'ruin'. E.g., 50% means ruin if balance drops to half of starting capital.

profitChartGuide.title: How to export from ProfitChart
profitChartGuide.autoDetect: Exports from ProfitChart are automatically detected and imported. Assets like WING26 are automatically normalized to WIN.
profitChartGuide.step1: Open the Performance Report: Menu > Trading > Performance Report
profitChartGuide.step2: Use the top filters to select account, asset, and period (use 'Custom' for specific date ranges)
profitChartGuide.step3: Switch to the 'Operations' tab (the 'Summary' tab exports as PDF, not CSV)
profitChartGuide.step4: Click the 'Export' button (spreadsheet icon) in the top-right corner
profitChartGuide.step5: Save the .csv file and upload it here
profitChartGuide.tip: If Day Trade mode is enabled, only trades opened and closed on the same day will be included.
profitChartGuide.columnsNote: Exported columns: Asset, Open/Close time, Quantity, Buy/Sell price, MEP (MFE), MEN (MAE), Operation Result, and more.

csv.requiredColumns: Your CSV should include these required columns:
csv.optionalColumns: Optional columns: exit_date, exit_price, stop_loss, take_profit, pnl, timeframe, notes, followed_plan

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Analyze your performance and identify patterns
analytics.expectancyTooltipR: Edge Expectancy — measures strategy quality in R-multiples. Account-size independent: 1R is 1R whether your account is $10K or $1M. Use this to evaluate your trading edge objectively.
analytics.expectancyTooltipCapital: Capital Expectancy — shows actual dollar P&L impact. Depends on position sizing: same edge produces different $ results at different risk levels.
analytics.expectancyTooltipDiffer: A strategy with great R performance but small position sizes will look strong in R but modest in $. Large positions amplify $ results even with a mediocre R edge.

tableTooltips.pnl: Net Profit & Loss (total gains minus losses)

tableTooltips.avgR: Average R-multiple per trade (actual return ÷ planned risk)
tableTooltips.pf: Profit Factor (sum of wins ÷ sum of losses)

upload.invalidFileType: Invalid file type. Accepted: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp

tags.nameMaxLength: Name must be 50 characters or less
tags.invalidColor: Color must be a valid hex color

tooltips.winRate: Percentage of winning trades out of total
tooltips.profitFactor: Sum of wins divided by sum of losses
tooltips.rMultiple: Result in terms of initial risk (P&L / Risk)
tooltips.mfe: Maximum Favorable Excursion - highest potential profit during trade
tooltips.mae: Maximum Adverse Excursion - highest potential loss during trade
tooltips.expectedValue: Average expected return per trade based on win rate and averages

execution.addDescription: Add a new entry or exit execution to this trade
execution.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this execution? This action cannot be undone.
execution.convertDescription: Convert this simple trade to a scaled position with multiple entries/exits

riskSimulation.dateRangeDesc: Select the time period containing the trades you want to simulate. The simulator will replay your actual trades using the risk rules you define.
riskSimulation.yearFilterDesc: Jump to a specific year or select all-time to include every trade in your history.
riskSimulation.prefillSelectorDesc: Choose where your risk parameters come from — your monthly plan, a saved risk profile, or enter them manually.
riskSimulation.riskParamsDesc: These are the rules the simulator will enforce: account balance, risk per trade, daily/weekly/monthly loss limits, and optional position sizing adjustments.
riskSimulation.reductionFactorDesc: After each consecutive loss, the simulator multiplies your risk per trade by this factor. Example with 50%: if your risk is R$500, after the 1st loss → R$250, 2nd loss → R$125, 3rd loss → R$62.50. Protects your capital during losing streaks.
riskSimulation.reinvestmentPercentDesc: After a winning trade, this percentage of the profit is added to your base risk for the next trade. Example with 30%: if your risk is R$500 and you profit R$1,000, next risk becomes R$500 + (30% × R$1,000) = R$800. Compounds gains while keeping losses at the original size.
riskSimulation.runSimulationDesc: Once your date range and parameters are set, click here to run the simulation and see how your trades would have performed under these risk rules.
riskSimulation.equityCurveDesc: Compare your original equity curve (actual results) against the simulated curve (with risk rules applied). The gap between lines shows the impact of proper risk management.
riskSimulation.decisionTraceDesc: View a week-by-week breakdown of every decision the simulator made — which trades were taken, skipped, or had their size adjusted, and why.

monteCarlo.tabsDesc: Monte Carlo has two modes. Edge Expectancy tests whether your win rate and reward:risk ratio produce a statistical edge across thousands of random sequences. Capital Expectancy simulates your actual capital growth over months, factoring in commissions, ruin thresholds, and compounding.
monteCarlo.dataSourceDesc: Pick which trades to analyze — a specific asset, strategy, or all trades. The simulator extracts your historical win rate and reward:risk ratio from these trades.
monteCarlo.useStatsDesc: After selecting a data source, click here to auto-fill the simulation parameters with your real trading statistics. You can also click Customize to enter values manually.
monteCarlo.winRateDesc: Core simulation inputs: win rate (% of trades that are profitable), reward:risk ratio (average win ÷ average loss), commission impact, number of trades per sequence, and how many sequences to run.
monteCarlo.simulationCountDesc: How many random trade sequences to generate. More simulations = smoother probability curves. Example: 1,000 simulations of 100 trades each = 100,000 simulated trades total.
monteCarlo.runSimulationDesc: Generates thousands of random trade sequences using your parameters. Each sequence shuffles win/loss outcomes at your win rate to show the range of possible results.
monteCarlo.equityCurveDesc: Shows all simulated equity paths overlaid. The spread between best and worst case reveals how much luck affects your results. A tight band means your edge is consistent; a wide band means high variance.
monteCarlo.drawdownDesc: Visualizes the worst peak-to-trough decline in each simulation. Example: if you start with R$10,000 and drawdown hits 30%, you dropped to R$7,000 before recovering. Helps set realistic stop-loss limits.
monteCarlo.distributionDesc: Histogram showing where most simulations end up. Example: if 80% of simulations end above R$10,000 starting capital, your edge has an 80% probability of being profitable over that many trades.
monteCarlo.metricsDesc: Key statistics from the simulation: edge summary (expected R per trade), profitability rate across all sequences, worst and best drawdowns, and confidence intervals. These numbers tell you how robust your strategy really is.
monteCarlo.kellyDesc: The mathematically optimal bet sizing to maximize long-term growth. Shows Full Kelly (aggressive), Half Kelly (balanced), and Quarter Kelly (conservative). Example: if Kelly says 10% and your capital is R$10,000, optimal risk per trade is R$1,000. Most traders use Half Kelly for safety.
monteCarlo.strategyDesc: AI-powered analysis of your simulation results. Evaluates your edge strength, risk profile, and provides actionable recommendations based on the statistical outcomes.
monteCarlo.v2InitialBalanceDesc: Your starting account balance for the capital simulation. Example: R$10,000. All growth, drawdowns, and ruin calculations are based on this starting point.
monteCarlo.v2RuinThresholdDesc: The account balance at which the simulation considers you 'ruined' and stops trading. Example: with R$10,000 initial and a R$2,000 ruin threshold, if your balance drops to R$2,000 (an 80% loss), the simulation stops that path.
monteCarlo.v2RunSimulationDesc: Simulates months of trading with your real capital, applying commissions, daily trade counts, and compounding. Shows the probability of reaching your goals vs. hitting the ruin threshold.
monteCarlo.v2DailyPnlDesc: Shows the daily profit and loss across simulated trading days. Reveals how your capital fluctuates day-to-day and helps identify if your strategy produces consistent income or volatile swings.
monteCarlo.v2DistributionDesc: Histogram of final capital across all simulations. Example: starting with R$10,000, if the median outcome is R$15,000 after 6 months, most paths grew 50%. The left tail shows ruin scenarios.
monteCarlo.v2SummaryDesc: Overview of the simulation parameters used — your risk profile, win rate, reward:risk ratio, and number of simulations. Click 'Run Again' to re-run with different parameters.
monteCarlo.v2ModeDistributionDesc: Pie chart showing the proportion of simulation paths that ended in profit vs. ruin vs. breakeven. Gives you an at-a-glance probability of each outcome.

analytics.filtersDesc: Filter your analytics by time period, asset, direction, outcome, or timeframe. The R/$ toggle switches all charts between edge mode (R-multiples) and capital mode (R$ values). Example: in edge mode, a 2R win means you gained twice your risk regardless of position size.
analytics.variableComparisonDesc: Compare P&L, win rate, average R, trade count, or profit factor across different groupings — by asset, timeframe, hour of day, day of week, or strategy. Example: group by 'Hour' with metric 'Win Rate' to find your most profitable trading hours.
analytics.equityCurveDesc: Your running total of profit and loss over time. An upward-sloping curve means consistent profitability. Flat periods indicate drawdowns or low activity. Example: if the curve rises R$5,000 over 3 months then drops R$2,000, your max drawdown during that period was R$2,000.
analytics.expectedValueDesc: How much you expect to make per trade on average. In edge mode, shows R-based expectancy: (Win Rate × Avg Win R) - (Loss Rate × Avg Loss R). In capital mode, shows R$ expectancy. Example: an EV of +0.3R means each trade, on average, nets 0.3× your risk — after 100 trades, that's +30R.
analytics.rDistributionDesc: Histogram showing how your trade outcomes distribute across R-multiple ranges. Reveals whether your profits come from many small wins or a few large ones. Example: if most trades cluster at -1R to +1R but a few reach +3R to +5R, your strategy relies on occasional big winners.
analytics.tagCloudDesc: Performance breakdown by your custom tags — setups, mistakes, and general labels. Shows which setups make money, which mistakes cost you the most, and the impact of each tag on your P&L. Example: if 'VWAP Bounce' setup has +R$5,000 across 40 trades but 'Revenge Trade' mistake costs -R$3,000, you know exactly where to focus.
analytics.timeSectionDesc: This section breaks down your performance by time dimensions — when you trade matters as much as how you trade. Includes a heatmap, session analysis, hourly patterns, and day-of-week performance.
analytics.heatmapDesc: Color-coded grid showing P&L (or R) by day of week and hour. Green cells are profitable time slots, red cells are losers. Example: if Monday 10:00-11:00 is dark green but Friday 15:00-16:00 is dark red, consider avoiding end-of-week afternoon trades.
analytics.sessionChartDesc: Compares your results across B3 trading sessions — Pre-Market, Morning, Afternoon, and After-Hours. Shows which session produces the best risk-adjusted returns. Example: if Morning session has +R$8,000 with 55% win rate but Afternoon has -R$2,000 with 40% win rate, your edge is clearly in the morning.
analytics.sessionAssetDesc: Cross-reference table showing how each asset performs in each trading session. Reveals optimal asset-session combinations. Example: WIN may perform well in the Morning session but poorly in the Afternoon, while stocks might be the opposite.
analytics.hourlyDesc: Bar chart showing P&L or R-multiple by hour of day. Identifies your peak and worst trading hours. Example: if 10:00 shows +R$3,000 across all trades but 14:00 shows -R$1,500, the market open is significantly better for your strategy.
analytics.dayOfWeekDesc: Bar chart showing P&L or R-multiple by trading day. Reveals weekly patterns in your results. Example: if Tuesday and Wednesday are consistently green but Friday is red, you might consider reducing size or sitting out on Fridays.

settings.tabsDesc: Settings is organized into tabs. Profile and Account are available to all users. Admin users see additional tabs for Conditions, Assets, Timeframes, and User Management.
settings.dataDisplayDesc: Controls how data is aggregated. 'Show All Accounts' combines trades from all your accounts in analytics and dashboard views. Example: if you have 3 accounts with 100 trades each, toggling this ON shows analytics for all 300 trades together.
settings.defaultFeesDesc: Default commission and fees applied to every new trade in this account. Example: if commission is R$0.50 and fees are R$0.30 per contract, a 10-contract trade costs R$8.00 in total fees. Breakeven ticks determine when a trade is classified as breakeven vs. loss.
settings.assetOverridesDesc: Override the default fees for specific assets. Example: if WINFUT has different exchange fees than PETR4, set custom commission and fees here. Assets without overrides use the account defaults above.
settings.dataMaintenanceDesc: Recalculate derived values across all trades. Use 'Recalculate R' after changing risk parameters, and 'Recalculate P&L' after updating fee structures. Both operations process all trades in this account.
settings.tagsDesc: Create and manage tags to categorize your trades. Three types: Setups (green — your entry strategies), Mistakes (red — errors to track), and General (blue — any other label). Tags appear in the trade form and power the Tag Analysis in Analytics.
settings.conditionsDesc: Define trading conditions that appear as checkboxes in the trade form. Categories: Indicator, Price Action, Market Context, and Custom. Conditions feed the Discipline Score on the dashboard.

accountComparison.selectorDesc: Choose which accounts to compare side-by-side. Select at least 2 accounts — each card shows the account name and type. Accounts typically share the same trades but have different risk configurations (commission, fees, risk per trade).
accountComparison.statsTableDesc: Side-by-side metrics for every selected account. The best value in each row is highlighted. Example: if Account A has Net P&L of R$10,000 and Account B has R$8,500, Account A is highlighted — showing that its lower commission saved R$1,500 over the same trades.
accountComparison.normalizedTableDesc: Scales monetary metrics to a common risk per trade, so accounts with different position sizes are directly comparable. Example: if Account A risks R$100/trade and Account B risks R$500/trade, raw P&L comparison is misleading — this table normalizes both to the same reference risk.
accountComparison.equityChartDesc: Overlaid equity curves for all selected accounts on the same chart. Shows how fee and risk differences compound over time. Example: two accounts starting at R$0 may diverge by R$5,000 after 200 trades purely due to different commission structures.
accountComparison.configSummaryDesc: Reference table showing each account's commission, fees, risk per trade, and account type. Helps you remember what differentiates each account in the comparison above.

dashboard.periodToggleDesc: Filter all dashboard data by time period. Month shows only the current month, Year shows the current year, and All Time includes your entire trading history.
dashboard.kpiCardsDesc: Your most important trading metrics at a glance: Net P&L, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Average R, Discipline Score, and trade counts. These update based on the period filter above.
dashboard.calendarDesc: Monthly view color-coded by daily P&L. Green days are profitable, red days are losses, gray days had no trades. Click any day to see its trades in detail. Navigate between months using the arrow buttons.
dashboard.quickStatsDesc: Current and best winning/losing streaks, plus key ratios. Example: a current winning streak of 5 means your last 5 trades were all profitable. Tracking streaks helps identify when you're in or out of rhythm.
dashboard.radarDesc: Spider chart scoring your trading across multiple dimensions: profitability, consistency, risk management, discipline, and volume. A balanced shape indicates well-rounded trading; spikes or dips reveal strengths and weaknesses.
dashboard.equityCurveDesc: Your cumulative P&L over time. An upward-sloping line means consistent profitability. Flat or declining periods indicate drawdowns. Example: if the curve rises R$5,000 over 3 months then drops R$2,000, your max drawdown was R$2,000.

journal.periodFilterDesc: Filter your trade journal by time period: Day (today only), Week (Mon-Sun), Month (current month), All (entire history), or Custom (pick specific dates). The journal reloads trades for the selected period.
journal.periodSummaryDesc: Quick overview of the filtered period: total trade count, net P&L, win/loss breakdown, and win rate percentage. Example: '42 trades R$+2,350 28W 14L (67%)' means 42 trades with R$2,350 net profit and 67% win rate.
journal.tradeGroupsDesc: Trades are grouped by trading day. Each group header shows the date, daily P&L, win/loss count, and win rate. Click the header to expand/collapse. Click any trade row to view full details and edit.

commandCenter.circuitBreakerDesc: Real-time status of your daily loss limit. When your losses reach the limit set in your monthly plan, the circuit breaker triggers and signals you to stop trading. Colors change from green (safe) to yellow (warning) to red (triggered).
commandCenter.liveTradingDesc: Shows real-time P&L for assets you traded today, updated from market data. Displays current session phase (pre-market, morning, afternoon, after-hours) and your position status for each active asset.
commandCenter.dailyChecklistDesc: A pre-trading routine you define. Check off items before the market opens to ensure you've completed your preparation. Example: 'Review key levels', 'Check economic calendar', 'Set daily max loss'. Completion feeds your Discipline Score.
commandCenter.planSummaryDesc: Shows your current monthly plan parameters at a glance: risk per trade and daily loss limit. If no plan exists, you'll see a prompt to create one. The plan feeds the circuit breaker and risk simulation.
commandCenter.assetRulesDesc: Per-asset trading rules that apply for the day. Set maximum contracts, session restrictions, or custom notes for specific assets. Example: 'WINFUT: max 5 contracts, morning session only'. Rules help enforce discipline during live trading.
commandCenter.dailySummaryDesc: Automated end-of-day performance card showing today's P&L, trade count, win rate, best/worst trades, and comparison against your plan targets. Appears after your first trade of the day.

monthly.navigatorDesc: Navigate between months to review your monthly performance. Use the arrows or click the month name to pick a specific month. Past months show final results; the current month shows live data.
monthly.profitSummaryDesc: Breakdown of your monthly profits. For personal accounts, shows gross and net P&L. For prop firm accounts, shows gross profit, your share after the profit split, and net after day-trade tax. Example: R$10,000 gross with 80% split = R$8,000 your share, minus 20% tax = R$6,400 net.
monthly.projectionDesc: Extrapolates your current month's performance to estimate the full-month result. Based on your daily average P&L and remaining trading days. Example: if your daily average is R$500 with 10 days remaining, projected additional profit is R$5,000. Only shown for the current month.
monthly.comparisonDesc: Side-by-side comparison of current month vs. previous month. Shows changes in profit, win rate, average R, and trade count with percentage differences. Green arrows mean improvement, red means decline.
monthly.weeklyBreakdownDesc: Splits the month into trading weeks showing P&L, trade count, and win rate for each week. Visual bars show each week's contribution to the monthly total. Helps identify if your performance was consistent or if one exceptional week skewed the results.

reports.weeklyDesc: Detailed report for the current trading week. Shows net P&L, win rate, trade count, profit factor, plus best and worst trades. Expand for daily breakdown with per-day P&L and win/loss counts. Navigate to previous weeks with the arrow buttons.
reports.monthlyDesc: Monthly performance report with summary metrics, best/worst trading days, and asset breakdown. Expand to see weekly P&L within the month and top-performing assets. Navigate between months to track long-term trends.
reports.mistakeCostDesc: Calculates the actual financial impact of each mistake tag in your trades. Shows total cost, most costly mistake type, and a ranked breakdown with percentage bars. Example: if 'Revenge Trade' tag appears on trades totaling -R$3,000, you know exactly how much that behavior costs you.

tradeDetail.metricsDesc: Key numbers for this trade: entry/exit prices, position size, and risk amount. The risk amount is calculated from your Stop Loss and determines the R-multiple. Example: if risk is R$100 and you made R$250, that's a +2.5R trade.
tradeDetail.positionSummaryDesc: Aggregated view of your position: average entry/exit prices (weighted by quantity), total points gained, position status (open/partial/closed), and realized P&L per contract. Example: 2 entries at 128,000 (3 contracts) and 127,950 (2 contracts) = avg entry 127,980. This card only appears for scaled trades.
tradeDetail.executionsDesc: Every individual fill (entry and exit) with timestamp, price, quantity, and order type. You can add, edit, or delete executions. For simple trades, you'll see an option to convert to scaled mode — useful when you need to add a scale-in or partial exit retroactively.
tradeDetail.riskAnalysisDesc: Visual bar comparing your planned R-target (from SL/TP at entry) vs. your actual realized R. Example: if you planned 2R but realized +3R, you outperformed your target. If planned 2R but realized -1R, the full stop was hit. This comparison reveals execution quality over time.
tradeDetail.excursionDesc: MFE shows the best price reached in your favor during the trade. MAE shows the worst price against you. Comparing MFE to your exit reveals if you left profit on the table. Comparing MAE to your Stop Loss shows if you were nearly stopped out. Example: MFE of R$128,200 but exit at R$128,050 means you missed R$150 of potential profit.
tradeDetail.classificationDesc: The strategy and tags assigned to this trade. Tags are grouped by type: Setups (green — entry strategies like 'VWAP Bounce'), Mistakes (yellow — errors like 'Revenge Trade'), and General (blue). These feed the Tag Analysis in Analytics and help you identify which patterns make or lose money.

newTrade.modeSelectorDesc: Choose between Simple (single entry/exit) and Scaled (multiple entries and exits). Scaled mode lets you record partial fills and scale in/out of positions. Both modes share journal, tags, and risk data — you can switch modes without losing your inputs.
newTrade.slTpDesc: The two most critical fields in every trade. Stop Loss defines your maximum acceptable loss — it determines your risk amount and R-multiple calculations across the entire app. Take Profit defines your target. Example: WINFUT long entry at 128,000 with SL at 127,950 and TP at 128,150 gives you a 3:1 reward-to-risk ratio (150pts reward / 50pts risk).
newTrade.plannedRiskDesc: Your monetary risk for this trade, calculated automatically from: (Entry Price - Stop Loss) × Position Size × (Tick Value / Tick Size). Example: entry 128,000, SL 127,950, 5 WINFUT contracts → (50pts / 5) × R$0.20 × 5 = R$10.00 at risk. This value feeds all R-multiple calculations in Analytics, Risk Simulation, and Monte Carlo.
newTrade.plannedRDesc: Your reward-to-risk ratio based on Take Profit and Stop Loss. Calculated as: (TP - Entry) / (Entry - SL) for longs. Example: if SL = 50pts away and TP = 150pts away, your R-target is 3.0R — meaning you aim to gain 3× what you risk. Tracking planned R helps you only take trades with favorable risk/reward.
newTrade.mfeMaeDesc: Maximum Favorable Excursion (MFE) is the highest price reached in your favor during the trade. Maximum Adverse Excursion (MAE) is the deepest price went against you. Example: long entry at 128,000, price reached 128,200 (MFE) before dropping to 127,900 (MAE) then exiting at 128,050. These reveal if you're exiting too early (MFE >> exit) or holding losers too long (MAE >> SL).
newTrade.contractsExecutedDesc: Total number of contracts bought and sold in this trade. Used to calculate commission and fees accurately. Default is position size × 2 (one buy + one sell). If you scaled in/out with multiple fills, enter the actual total. Example: bought 3 then 2 more, sold all 5 = 10 contracts executed. This directly affects your net P&L calculation.
newTrade.setupRankDesc: Rate the quality of this trade setup before entering. A = meets minimum criteria, AA = strong setup with confluence, AAA = textbook setup. Appears after selecting a strategy. Tracking setup quality helps identify which setups deserve larger position sizes.
newTrade.pnlSummaryDesc: Real-time P&L calculated from your entry/exit prices, position size, and account fees. Shows net P&L (after costs), ticks gained, and R-multiple. Example: +R$150.00 (+1.5R) means you made R$150 which is 1.5× your initial risk.
newTrade.scaledEntriesDesc: Add multiple entry rows to record scaling into a position. Each row has date, time, price, quantity, and commission. The weighted average entry price is calculated automatically. Example: 3 contracts at 128,000 + 2 contracts at 127,950 = average entry 127,980.
newTrade.scaledExitsDesc: Add exit rows to record partial or full exits. If total exit quantity equals entry quantity, the position is 'closed'. If less, it shows as 'partial'. Example: entered 5 contracts, exited 3 = partial position with 2 still open.
newTrade.positionSummaryDesc: Real-time aggregation of all entries and exits. Shows position status (open/partial/closed), entry and exit counts with quantities, total commission, and net P&L with R-multiple when the position is partially or fully closed.
newTrade.scaledRiskDesc: Same as simple mode but risk is calculated from the weighted average entry price. The Risk Amount updates dynamically as you add/remove entry executions. Example: adding a lower entry to a long position reduces average entry, which increases distance to SL and therefore increases risk.
newTrade.csvUploadDesc: Drag and drop or select a CSV file exported from your trading platform. Required columns: asset, direction, entry_date, entry_price, position_size. Optional columns include exit data, SL/TP, fees, and MFE/MAE. Download the template to see the expected format.
newTrade.csvSummaryDesc: After upload, trades are validated against your configured assets. Green = valid and ready to import, yellow = valid with warnings (review recommended), red = skipped due to errors (e.g., unknown asset). Use filters to focus on specific statuses. Select/deselect trades before importing.
newTrade.csvSlTpDesc: Bulk-generate missing Stop Loss and Take Profit values for imported trades that don't have them. Uses the asset's tick configuration to calculate reasonable SL/TP levels based on your historical average risk. Saves time when importing large batches from platforms that don't export SL/TP.

newStrategy.rulesCriteriaDesc: Document your exact entry and exit criteria. These are the rules you commit to following for this strategy. When you log a trade and mark 'Followed Plan: No', the compliance score for this strategy drops. Clear, specific rules make it easier to evaluate discipline. Example: Entry — 'Price bounces off VWAP with volume spike above 2x average'. Exit — 'First target at 1.5R, trail stop at breakeven after 1R'.
newStrategy.riskSettingsDesc: Target R-Multiple sets the minimum reward you aim for with this strategy. Max Risk Per Trade caps position size as a percentage of your account. Example: Target R of 2.0 means you only take this setup when the reward is at least 2× your risk. Max risk of 1% on a R$50,000 account = R$500 max risk per trade. These values appear on the strategy detail page for quick reference.
newStrategy.conditionsDesc: Attach pre-defined market conditions to this strategy in three tiers: Must Have (required for entry — e.g., 'Trend aligned with daily bias'), Should Have (strongly preferred — e.g., 'Volume above average'), and Nice to Have (bonus confluence — e.g., 'Round number nearby'). When logging trades, checking these conditions calculates the compliance score automatically.

strategyDetail.performanceDesc: Live statistics from all trades tagged with this strategy. Win Rate shows consistency, Profit Factor measures gross wins ÷ gross losses (above 1.5 is strong), Avg R shows edge per trade, and Compliance tracks how often you followed your own rules. Example: 65% win rate with 1.8 profit factor and +0.5R avg means this strategy has a consistent statistical edge.
strategyDetail.conditionsDesc: Shows the conditions attached to this strategy organized by importance: Must Have (red if missing — trade should not be taken), Should Have (yellow — weakens the setup), Nice to Have (blue — extra confluence). When you log a trade, checking these conditions determines the compliance percentage for that trade. 100% compliance = all 'Must Have' and 'Should Have' conditions were met.

playbook.complianceDesc: Dashboard showing how well you're following your defined strategies. Tracks compliance rate, most and least followed strategies, and overall discipline trends. High compliance correlates with more consistent results.
playbook.strategiesDesc: Your collection of trading strategies (playbook entries). Each card shows the strategy name, code, win rate, average R, and trade count. Click to view full details, rules, and performance history. Use 'New Strategy' to document a new setup.

accountComparison.title: Account Comparison
accountComparison.subtitle: Compare performance across accounts with different risk configurations

normalized.title: Normalized Comparison
normalized.description: Monetary metrics scaled to a common risk per trade, so accounts with different position sizes are comparable in R$ terms.
